ogTitle: Central Mangrove vs Cessnock: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Central Mangrove and Cessnock.
heading: Central Mangrove vs Cessnock — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Central Mangrove, New South Wales and Cessnock, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Central Mangrove has a lower overall climate risk score (39/100) compared to Cessnock (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Central Mangrove or Cessnock safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Central Mangrove has a flood risk score of 26/100, while Cessnock scores 46/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Central Mangrove scores 43/100 for bushfire risk. Cessnock scores 40/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Central Mangrove or Cessnock?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Central Mangrove has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.82% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
